moat.description: QuickBooks (58% of revenue) is the system of record for 7M+ SMBs — though AI-native platforms have begun siphoning off tech-forward customers. TurboTax's primary regulatory headwind (IRS Direct File) was cancelled for 2026, partially reversing the bear case.
moat.analysisSummary: Intuit's moat is **revenue-weighted**: QuickBooks (GBSG) dominates at ~58% of FY2026's guided ~$21B, TurboTax ~26%, Credit Karma ~12%, ProTax ~3%. IRS Direct File cancellation for 2026 eliminates the primary near-term TurboTax regulatory headwind.

analysisPoints.title: QuickBooks: The Real Fortress (58%)
analysisPoints.text: 7M+ SMBs use QuickBooks as their system of record for payroll, invoicing, taxes, and lending. Years of embedded transaction history make migration nearly impossible — this is the dominant moat driver. AI-native competitors (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) are siphoning off tech-forward SMBs but have not yet demonstrated the ability to displace QuickBooks at scale across the installed base.

analysisPoints.title: TurboTax: IRS Direct File Cancelled (26%)
analysisPoints.text: The Trump administration cancelled the IRS Direct File programme for the 2026 filing season, removing the primary near-term headwind to TurboTax's addressable market. TurboTax Live (human-assisted filing) grew 47% in FY2025 and now represents 41% of Consumer revenue — a successful pivot to a more defensible, higher-ARPU model that AI point-solutions cannot easily replicate.

analysisPoints.title: Data Flywheel Across All Segments
analysisPoints.text: 100M+ users' tax returns, SMB financials, and Credit Karma credit profiles create an irreplaceable dataset that improves underwriting, product personalisation, and AI model training across the platform. Intuit Assist (AI copilot) is being monetised across QuickBooks and TurboTax — this data flywheel is the most durable long-term defence against AI-native entrants who lack the historical financial depth.

growth.description: Q2 FY2026 revenue grew 17% YoY to $4.7B, with Global Business Solutions (QuickBooks) up 18% (+21% ex-Mailchimp) and Non-GAAP EPS of $4.15 (+25% YoY). Full-year FY2026 guidance reiterated at $20.997–21.186B (+12–13%). IRS Direct File cancellation removes the primary TurboTax headwind entering FY2027, while AI-native competitors (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) and Anthropic Managed Agents represent new headwinds to QuickBooks SMB growth. Margins guided to continue expanding.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 67 (8–15% CAGR, FY2026 guided 12–13%) + 5 recurring subscription (QuickBooks ARPU NRR >110%, 7M+ sticky SMBs) + 5 TAM expansion (Intuit Assist AI monetisation, mid-market Enterprise Suite, international) + 4 IRS Direct File cancelled (near-term TurboTax volume re-acceleration in FY2027) = 81

drivers.name: Global Business Solutions (QuickBooks)
drivers.metric: +18% YoY Q2 FY26; +21% ex-Mailchimp; QBO Advanced +40% YoY

drivers.name: Consumer Tax (TurboTax Live)
drivers.metric: TurboTax Live +47% in FY2025; 41% of Consumer revenue; IRS Direct File cancelled

drivers.name: Credit Karma
drivers.metric: +12% expected FY2026; rate normalisation tailwind; $2.5B+ annual revenue run-rate

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: If AI-native SMB platforms (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) achieve >5% of the US tech SMB market by end of 2027, QuickBooks mid-market expansion stalls and ARPU growth falls below 5% — making the 12–13% FY2026 revenue guidance the ceiling rather than a floor

valuation.description: At ~$351, trading 32% below the base case ($520) and just 21% above the bear ($290). The forward non-GAAP P/E of ~14.8× and PEG of ~1.0× represent the lowest valuation since 2020 — the market appears to be pricing in near-permanent AI disruption of both TurboTax and QuickBooks, a scenario that materially overstates execution risk given the IRS Direct File cancellation and 7M+ SMB switching costs.

peAnalysis.summary: At ~23× forward GAAP P/E — near the lowest multiple since 2020 — Intuit now trades at or below the S&P 500 software median despite a demonstrably superior moat profile. The non-GAAP PEG of ~1.0× signals genuine GARP territory, implying the market has over-discounted AI disruption risk relative to the underlying earnings power. The trailing-to-forward P/E compression (27× to 23×) reflects an accelerating earnings trajectory that should widen further as IRS Direct File removal boosts FY2027 TurboTax volumes and QuickBooks pricing power compounds.

bear.description: AI-native SMB platforms gain critical traction, QuickBooks churn accelerates beyond management's ability to offset with pricing, and the multiple stays permanently compressed.
points: AI-native platforms (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) capture >10% of US tech SMBs by end of 2027, materially accelerating QuickBooks churn in the highest-ARPU growth segment
SMB failures in tariff-affected sectors add 2–3 pp of quarterly churn above historical norms across the 7M+ installed base
Credit Karma revenue recovery stalls as consumer lending conditions remain tight; segment drags total growth below 10% and the GAAP P/E stays at 14–15× on stagnant EPS

base.description: Company executes on FY2026 guidance, Intuit Assist monetises at scale, TurboTax volumes recover with IRS Direct File gone, and the multiple re-rates modestly as AI disruption fears prove overdone.
points: FY2026 revenue lands at guidance midpoint (~$21.1B, +13% YoY); FY2027 re-accelerates to 14–15% as TurboTax volumes recover with IRS Direct File permanently eliminated
Intuit Assist AI copilot achieves meaningful paid penetration, driving QuickBooks ARPU above $2,000 per customer as mid-market Enterprise Suite gains traction
Non-GAAP P/E re-rates from 14.8× to ~19–20× as AI disruption narrative moderates; FY2027 non-GAAP EPS of ~$26 × 20× ≈ $520

bull.description: Intuit Assist becomes the AI financial operating system for SMBs globally, TurboTax market share expands as Direct File is permanently eliminated, and the multiple re-rates toward historical norms.
points: International QuickBooks expansion reaches critical mass in UK, Canada, and ANZ; total addressable SMB market effectively doubles and international mix reaches 20%+ of GBSG
Intuit Assist monetisation exceeds expectations — AI-assisted premium tasks priced above base subscription; non-GAAP EPS exceeds $28–30 by FY2027–28
Multiple re-rates to 26–28× non-GAAP earnings as AI-native threat proves manageable and IRS Direct File remains cancelled; $28 × 27× ≈ $756

learnedInterfaces.note: TurboTax's guided interview format is being challenged by AI tax assistants that offer conversational, agent-driven filing. QuickBooks' UI is increasingly abstracted by agentic automation layers.

businessLogic.note: AI-native competitors (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) and Anthropic's Managed Agents (April 2026) directly target QuickBooks bookkeeping workflows — claiming to automate up to 98% of bookkeeping tasks. IRS Direct File cancellation removes one AI-assisted government competitor from TurboTax's market, but the broader agentic AI threat to accounting and tax logic persists.

publicDataAccess.note: Tax forms and IRS regulations are public — AI can navigate them, reducing Intuit's simplification value proposition for straightforward returns.

talentScarcity.note: AI has democratised access to tax and accounting expertise, reducing the barrier that made TurboTax's guided logic and QuickBooks' support structure so valuable.

bundling.note: TurboTax + QuickBooks + Credit Karma + Mailchimp = a unique SMB financial platform covering taxes, accounting, credit, and marketing. No AI point-solution replicates this breadth of financial data and workflow integration.

proprietaryData.note: Tax return data, financial records, and credit data for 100M+ users = an irreplaceable financial profile dataset powering Intuit Assist AI. Competitors building AI-native products lack the historical financial depth to train equivalent models.

regulatoryLockIn.note: IRS authorised e-file partner status, state tax agency relationships, payroll tax compliance, and financial institution integrations. IRS Direct File programme cancelled by the Trump administration for the 2026 filing season — eliminates the primary near-term regulatory headwind to TurboTax's addressable market.

networkEffects.note: CPA-client ecosystem: accountants recommend TurboTax → clients use it → data improves. QuickBooks SMB network self-reinforces as more suppliers and customers transact within the ecosystem.

transactionEmbedding.note: Payroll runs, invoicing, tax filing, expense tracking, and lending all embedded in the daily operations of 7M+ small businesses. Removing QuickBooks requires re-engineering core financial workflows — a multi-month migration with material business risk.

systemOfRecord.note: QuickBooks remains the system of record for 7M+ SMBs, but AI-native platforms (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) are siphoning off tech-forward customers who find manual categorisation outdated. Downgraded from strong to intact as the displacement threat has materialised in specific high-growth customer segments, though the 7M+ installed base remains deeply embedded.

tenMoats.verdict: Intuit is a mixed AI story: proprietary tax and financial data, transaction embedding, and network effects are genuinely AI-resilient moats, while the system-of-record position has been downgraded to intact as AI-native SMB platforms (Puzzle, Digits, Campfire) demonstrate real-world traction with tech-forward customers. The IRS Direct File cancellation removes the primary near-term TurboTax headwind and is a meaningful structural positive. The platform's 100M+ user financial data flywheel — spanning tax returns, SMB financials, and Credit Karma credit profiles — remains the most durable long-term defence against AI-native disruption.
